insights.title: Create custom peak meter visuals by drawing paths directly from the DisplayBuffer
insights.summary: The recommended pattern for a custom peak meter UI is to use a `core.peak` node with its display buffer, then draw graphics directly from that buffer in a ScriptPanel LAF function. This avoids the timer-polling approach and provides smooth, accurate metering. Both ustk and orange (trusted) confirm this is the preferred method.
insights.verify_hint: Verify DisplayBuffer path-drawing API and that core.peak exposes a display buffer for external reading.

insights.title: MatrixPeakMeter ChannelIndexes must be an array
insights.summary: When configuring a MatrixPeakMeter FloatingTile, the `ChannelIndexes` property must be a JSON array of zero-based integers, e.g. `[0, 1]` for stereo. A common mistake is passing a single integer or a one-based index which causes the meter to fail silently.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm ChannelIndexes JSON format requirement in the FloatingTile property editor and HISE source.

insights.title: Peak meter floating tile shows same value for both channels in compiled FX plugin
insights.summary: A confirmed bug was reported where the MatrixPeakMeter displayed the same peak value for both L and R channels in a compiled effect plugin, even with panned audio. The workaround is to use the `ENABLE_ALL_PEAK_METERS=1` compile definition. The root cause was the track panner being post-effect.
insights.verify_hint: Test compiled FX plugin with ENABLE_ALL_PEAK_METERS=1 and verify channel-independent peak readings.

insights.title: Use core.peak as an envelope follower trigger for ADSR gates
insights.summary: To use an audio signal's amplitude as a trigger for a gate-driven ADSR, connect a `core.peak` node's output to the gate parameter. For better smoothing, prefer `envelope_follower` over `core.peak` since it has dedicated attack/release controls. A `cable_expr` node can be inserted to define a custom threshold formula before the gate.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm core.peak outputs a normalised 0-1 value suitable for modulating a gate parameter.

insights.title: MatrixPeakMeter maxPeaks array always returns [0, 0]
insights.summary: A bug was identified where the `maxPeaks` array in the MatrixPeakMeter LAF callback always contains `[0, 0]` regardless of actual peak levels. David Healey identified this as a typo in the source and submitted a fix via PR.
insights.verify_hint: Check if the maxPeaks typo fix has been merged and the array now correctly reports peak-hold values.

insights.title: Peak metering in compiled plugins requires the ENABLE_ALL_PEAK_METERS compile definition
insights.summary: Peak meters (including the MatrixPeakMeter FloatingTile and timer-based getCurrentLevel() approaches) do not work in compiled plugins by default. You must add `ENABLE_ALL_PEAK_METERS=1` to your extra definitions in the project settings. An additional `ENABLE_PEAK_METERS_FOR_GAIN_EFFECT=1` flag exists for gain-effect-specific metering.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm both compile definitions and their effect on peak meter availability in exported plugins.
